task_summary: Implement a two-tier shot group system on the video editor timeline. The current `useShotGroups` inference becomes "suggested" groups (dashed border). Users can promote these to "pinned" groups persisted in TimelineConfig, enabling grouped drag, image↔video toggle with snapshot/restore, auto-sync with shot image changes, and context menu actions (Pin/Unpin, Switch to Video/Images). PinnedShotGroup data must survive save/load cycles and integrate with the undo system.

key_evidence.point: TimelineConfig has strict serialization validation: ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS in serialize.ts:98 only allows 'output', 'clips', 'tracks', 'customEffects'. Adding pinnedShotGroups requires updating this set, serializeForDisk(), and validateSerializedConfig().

TimelineData is the runtime type holding config, rows, meta, registry. pinnedShotGroups should be added to TimelineData AND TimelineConfig for persistence. Config goes through serializeForDisk → saveTimeline → loadTimeline → buildTimelineData round-trip.

useShotGroups currently infers groups from clip adjacency, generation ID matching against shot images, and a 0.5s MAX_GAP threshold. Returns ShotGroup with shotId, shotName, rowId, rowIndex, clipIds, color. This becomes the 'suggested' tier.

useSwitchToFinalVideo replaces image clips with a single video clip in one applyEdit call. It does NOT snapshot the original clips for swap-back. The pinned group system needs to snapshot clipIds+meta before swap and restore on switch-back.

Clip drag supports multi-select via DragSession.draggedClipIds with offset-based movement. Grouped drag for pinned groups can leverage this existing multi-drag infrastructure (planMultiDragMoves, applyMultiDragMoves).
ShotGroupContextMenu currently has ShotGroupMenuState with x,y,shotId,shotName,clipIds,rowId,hasFinalVideo. Needs 'isPinned' field added for Pin/Unpin toggle and mode-aware menu items.

Shot group rendering in TimelineCanvas uses PositionedShotGroup with pixel coordinates. Visual distinction (dashed vs solid border) applies here. The border is currently set as a plain 'border' class at line 769.

External drop flow (useExternalDrop) dispatches generation drops through handleAssetDrop which creates clips on tracks. Shot drops from shot list need a new handler that creates multiple image clips as a pinned group.

ShotsContext provides shots with images array. useShotImages/useAllShotGenerations loads shot_generations with React Query + realtimeBacked preset. Pinned group auto-sync can subscribe to shot image changes via these hooks.

applyEdit accepts TimelineEditMutation with type 'rows' (row/meta changes) or 'config' (full config rebuild). Pinned group mutations that only change pinnedShotGroups metadata (not clips) could use a lightweight path, but clip-affecting operations need full applyEdit.

Existing tests in useShotGroups.test.ts cover inference logic with 7 cases. These must continue passing (suggested groups unchanged). New tests needed for pinned group suppression of inferred groups for same shot+track.

relevant_code.why: Core inference logic that becomes 'suggested' tier. Needs to accept pinnedShotGroups param and suppress inferred groups where pinned exists for same shot+track.
TimelineConfig type definition — must add optional pinnedShotGroups field.
TimelineData type — must add pinnedShotGroups field. assembleTimelineData and buildTimelineData need to pass through pinnedShotGroups.
ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS must include 'pinnedShotGroups'. serializeForDisk must include it. validateSerializedConfig must allow it.
Must be refactored to work with pinned groups: auto-pin first, snapshot image clips into PinnedShotGroup.imageClipSnapshot, then swap to video.
Shot group rendering — must add isPinned to PositionedShotGroup, render dashed border for suggested vs solid for pinned, show shot name for pinned.
Context menu needs Pin/Unpin, Switch to Video/Images, and mode-aware items based on isPinned state.
Needs grouped drag for pinned groups: when dragging a clip that belongs to a pinned group, auto-select all group clips for multi-drag.
Needs shot-drop handler: dropping a shot from shot list creates pinned group with image clips expanded.
Orchestrates shot groups — must pass pinnedShotGroups from TimelineData to useShotGroups and TimelineCanvas.
applyEdit and materializeData must preserve pinnedShotGroups through edit cycles.
buildDataFromCurrentRegistry must pass pinnedShotGroups through rebuild.
Existing tests must pass. New tests needed for pinned group suppression.
handleAssetDrop creates clips — shot drop handler will need similar clip creation logic for multiple images.
Shot data source for auto-sync when images added/removed from a shot with pinned group.
Shot image data for populating pinned groups and detecting image changes.

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Basic inference still works — adjacent clips in shot order group together

test_expectations.test_id: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts::does not group clips when gap exceeds threshold
src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts::does not group clips when order disagrees
src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts::does not create groups for single clip

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Graceful handling of missing gen IDs unchanged
Timeline config serialization works with new pinnedShotGroups field

constraints: All 8 existing useShotGroups tests must continue to pass unchanged
Timeline config serialization must handle pinnedShotGroups in ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS, serializeForDisk, and validateSerializedConfig
Pinned groups must survive save/load/conflict-retry round-trips (DataProvider.saveTimeline → loadTimeline)
Undo/redo must work with pinned group operations since they go through applyEdit
useShotGroups inference must suppress inferred groups when a pinned group exists for the same shot+track
Switch-to-video must snapshot image clips in PinnedShotGroup.imageClipSnapshot before swapping, so switch-back restores them
Grouped drag must use existing multi-drag infrastructure (planMultiDragMoves/applyMultiDragMoves), not a separate drag system
No hardcoded colors — dashed/solid border distinction must use existing color-mix pattern from TimelineCanvas
Image↔video toggle must be a single applyEdit call in each direction for clean undo
AI timeline agent (supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/) reads TimelineConfig — adding pinnedShotGroups must not break its parsing
suggested_approach:
**Phase 1: Data model** — Add `PinnedShotGroup` type and `pinnedShotGroups?: PinnedShotGroup[]` to `TimelineConfig` (types/index.ts), `TimelineData` (timeline-data.ts). Update serialize.ts: add to `ALLOWED_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS`, pass through in `serializeForDisk`, allow in `validateSerializedConfig`. Thread through `assembleTimelineData`, `buildTimelineData`, `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`, and `rowsToConfig`.

**Phase 2: Inference suppression** — Modify `useShotGroups` to accept `pinnedShotGroups` param. Build a Set of `${shotId}:${trackId}` keys from pinned groups. Skip inferred groups matching those keys. Add `isPinned: boolean` to `ShotGroup` interface so downstream code can distinguish. Return merged array: pinned groups (from config) + filtered inferred groups.

**Phase 3: Visual distinction** — Add `isPinned` to `PositionedShotGroup` in TimelineCanvas. Render dashed border (`border-dashed`) for suggested, solid for pinned. Show shot name label always for pinned (not just on hover). Keep existing color-mix pattern.

**Phase 4: Pin/Unpin via context menu** — Add `isPinned` to `ShotGroupMenuState`. Add "Pin as Shot Group" / "Unpin" menu items. Pin action: read current clipIds from the inferred group, write `PinnedShotGroup` to config via `applyEdit`. Unpin: remove from config via `applyEdit`. Create a `usePinnedShotGroups` hook encapsulating pin/unpin/update mutations.

**Phase 5: Switch-to-video refactor** — Modify `useSwitchToFinalVideo` to auto-pin first if not already pinned. Before swapping, snapshot image clip metas into `PinnedShotGroup.imageClipSnapshot`. On switch-back, restore from snapshot. Both directions as single `applyEdit` calls.

**Phase 6: Grouped drag** — In `useClipDrag`, on pointer-down check if clip belongs to a pinned group. If so, auto-select all pinned group clipIds into `draggedClipIds`. This leverages existing multi-drag path with `planMultiDragMoves`/`applyMultiDragMoves`.

**Phase 7: Shot drop** — In `useExternalDrop`, detect shot-type drag data. On drop, create image clips for all shot images using `handleAssetDrop` pattern, then write a `PinnedShotGroup` entry. Single `applyEdit` for atomic creation.

**Phase 8: Auto-sync** — Subscribe to `ShotsContext` changes. When images added/removed from a shot that has a pinned group, update the pinned group's clipIds and add/remove corresponding timeline clips via `applyEdit`.
